creator: andrewb@cs.mcgill.ca
text:
Hi Jun,

There does not seem to be much difference once I added the debug flag:

This GDB was configured as "i386-marcel-freebsd"...
Core was generated by `amandad'.
Program terminated with signal 11, Segmentation fault.
Reading symbols from /usr/local/lib/libamandad-2.5.1p2.so...done.
Loaded symbols for /usr/local/lib/libamandad-2.5.1p2.so
Reading symbols from /usr/local/lib/libamanda-2.5.1p2.so...done.
Loaded symbols for /usr/local/lib/libamanda-2.5.1p2.so
Reading symbols from /lib/libm.so.4...done.
Loaded symbols for /lib/libm.so.4
Reading symbols from /lib/libreadline.so.6...done.
Loaded symbols for /lib/libreadline.so.6
Reading symbols from /lib/libncurses.so.6...done.
Loaded symbols for /lib/libncurses.so.6
Reading symbols from /lib/libc.so.6...done.
Loaded symbols for /lib/libc.so.6
Reading symbols from /libexec/ld-elf.so.1...done.
Loaded symbols for /libexec/ld-elf.so.1
#0  0x080498d3 in do_sendpkt (handle=0x0, pkt=0xbfbfe8c0) at
amandad.c:1594
1594        return security_sendpkt(handle, pkt);
(gdb) bt
#0  0x080498d3 in do_sendpkt (handle=0x0, pkt=0xbfbfe8c0) at
amandad.c:1594
#1  0x0804b4f9 in process_readnetfd (cookie=0x805b048) at amandad.c:1222
#2  0x2808aaac in event_loop_wait (wait_eh=0x0, dontblock=0) at
event.c:530
#3  0x0804967a in main (argc=1, argv=0xbfbfec50) at amandad.c:468

Let me know if there is anything else I can try.

A.

On Thu, 18 Jan 2007, Jun Kuriyama wrote:

> From: Jun Kuriyama <kuriyama@FreeBSD.org>
> Message-ID: <200701181314.l0IDE0OD025475@freefall.freebsd.org>
> Date: Thu, 18 Jan 2007 13:14:00 GMT
> To: andrewb@cs.mcgill.ca, kuriyama@FreeBSD.org, kuriyama@FreeBSD.org
> Subject: Re: ports/106685: misc/amanda-client: amanda-client-2.5.1p2,
>     1 backup failures (both dump and tar)
>
> Synopsis: misc/amanda-client: amanda-client-2.5.1p2,1 backup failures
(both dump and tar)
>
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: kuriyama
> State-Changed-When: Thu Jan 18 13:12:38 UTC 2007
> State-Changed-Why:
> I'm using this version on several clients, but it works fine.
>
> Can you take a core (and get backtrace by gdb) for this "signal 11"?
>
> http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/query-pr.cgi?pr=106685
>
